title: Bartsia mutica
taxonomy: Bartsia mutica is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Bartsia mutica is native to North America and is commonly found in moist, woodland areas. The plant produces small, white or purple flowers that are often clustered together. It is a relatively small plant, typically growing to be around 30-60 cm tall.

intro: Bartsia mutica, also known as Yellow Rattle, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Bartsia genus of the Orobanchaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its various health benefits, including its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bartsia mutica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
